title: Commentary on Genesis
author: St. Ephrem the Syrian (c. 306–373)
edition: Theosis editorial English rendering from the 1901 Russian translation of Ephrem's prose Commentary on Genesis. Russian text published by Holy Trinity-Sergius Lavra (1901), reprinted by Otchij Dom (Moscow, 1995, Творения, vol. 6); itself based on the mid-19th-century Moscow Theological Academy translation. English by Theosis from the Russian original, 2026.
originalDate: c. 360s — Ephrem's prose commentary on Genesis, composed in Nisibis or Edessa
copyrightNote: The Russian translation was published in 1901 by the Holy Trinity-Sergius Lavra and is therefore public domain worldwide (pre-1929 publication). The mid-19th-century Moscow Theological Academy translation on which it is based is itself public domain. The English rendering offered here is original work by Theosis, prepared from the public-domain Russian text. Original 4th-century Syriac is in the public domain. No copyrighted Mathews/Amar 1994 FOTC translation has been used.
structure: 50 chapter-commentaries on Genesis 1-50 plus a closing 'Conclusion.' Two chapters (33 and 36) are recorded with the explicit note 'No commentary is given on this chapter' (Толкования нет) — Ephrem himself passed over them. The text proceeds as flowing prose within each chapter, with Scripture citations placed inline.
